content:
# Staff Training Status Tracker

**Tool ID:** T019-01
**Block:** B019 (Food Safety Training Plan)
**Domain:** Food Safety & Hygiene (D01_food_safety)
**Level:** L1
**Format:** Tracker
**Role:** Manager
**Time Required:** 5-10 minutes per entry
**Version:** 1.0

---
## Welcome

This is a foundational tool -- the kind that prevents problems before they start. If a VMVT inspector asks "show me your training records," this is what you hand them.

What gets measured gets managed. This tracker turns invisible patterns into visible data you can act on.

**Built for Manager working in Food Safety & Hygiene.**
Use it. Adapt it. Make it yours.

---

## Purpose

Central tracker listing every team member with their Level 2 certification status, certificate details, expiry/refresh date, induction completion, and any additional training completed. One place, all staff, always current.

This tool supports **Food Safety Training Plan** (B019) and is designed to be practical, printable, and immediately useful in a working cafe environment.

---

## How to Use This Tool

**Who:** Manager
**Time Required:** 5-10 minutes per entry; 15 minutes for monthly review
**Frequency:** Update within 24 hours of any training event. Full review monthly.

**What You Will Need:**
- This tracker (spreadsheet or printed)
- Staff food hygiene certificates (originals or copies)
- Induction sign-off records
- Training session attendance records

**Steps:**
1. Add every current team member to the tracker -- no exceptions
2. For each person, fill in their certificate details from the original document
3. Calculate refresh dates (Level 2 refresh every 3 years)
4. Highlight anyone overdue or within 3 months of expiry in amber
5. Update immediately when new training is completed
6. Review the full tracker on the first Monday of each month

---

## Tracking Log

### Staff Training Register

| # | Staff Name | Role | Start Date | Level 2 Certificate | Provider | Cert Date | Refresh Due | Status | Induction Complete? | Induction Date | Allergen Training | Other Training | Notes |
|---|-----------|------|------------|---------------------|----------|-----------|-------------|--------|--------------------|--------------|--------------------|----------------|-------|
| 1 | *e.g., Marta K.* | *Barista* | *2024-03-01* | *Yes* | *Maisto higiena LT* | *2024-02-15* | *2027-02-15* | *Current* | *Yes* | *2024-03-01* | *2024-03-15* | *First Aid 2024-06* | |
| 2 | | | | | | | | | | | | | |
| 3 | | | | | | | | | | | | | |
| 4 | | | | | | | | | | | | | |
| 5 | | | | | | | | | | | | | |
| 6 | | | | | | | | | | | | | |
| 7 | | | | | | | | | | | | | |
| 8 | | | | | | | | | | | | | |
| 9 | | | | | | | | | | | | | |
| 10 | | | | | | | | | | | | | |

**Status Key:**
- Current = Certificate valid, all training up to date
- Expiring = Certificate expires within 3 months -- book renewal now
- Expired = Certificate has expired -- cannot work unsupervised in food handling until renewed
- Booked = Training course booked, date: _____
- Pending = New starter, training not yet arranged -- must be booked within first week

### Monthly Summary

| Month | Total Staff | Fully Current | Expiring (within 3 months) | Expired / Missing | New Starters Inducted | Training Sessions Held |
|-------|-----------|---------------|---------------------------|-------------------|----------------------|----------------------|
| *e.g., Mar 2026* | *8* | *6* | *1* | *1* | *0* | *1 (allergen refresher)* |
| | | | | | | |
| | | | | | | |
| | | | | | | |

### Expiry Alert Dashboard

| Staff Member | Certificate Type | Expiry Date | Days Remaining | Action Required | Owner |
|-------------|-----------------|-------------|----------------|-----------------|-------|
| | | | | | |
| | | | | | |
| | | | | | |

**Tip:** If more than 20% of your team has Expiring or Expired status, you have a systemic scheduling problem. Book a group training session rather than individual courses -- it is cheaper and builds team culture.

---

## Common Risks

### Risk 1: New Starter Works Without Certificate

**Why it happens:** Urgent hire to cover a leaving staff member. Manager plans to "sort training later." Weeks pass. Inspector arrives.
**How to prevent:** Add training booking to the recruitment process (T067-01). No solo food handling shifts until certificate confirmed.
**If it is already happening:** Book the course today. In the meantime, the new starter can only work under direct supervision of a certified team member.

### Risk 2: Tracker Not Updated When Staff Leave

**Why it happens:** Staff member leaves, but their row remains showing "Current." Tracker looks healthier than reality. Or worse -- their certificate is still counted toward compliance.
**How to prevent:** When processing a leaver, immediately mark their row as "Left" with the leaving date. Do not delete -- archive for records.
**If it is already happening:** Cross-reference the tracker against your current rota right now. Remove anyone not currently employed.

### Risk 3: Allergen Training Treated as Optional

**Why it happens:** Level 2 covers allergens briefly, so the manager assumes separate allergen training is unnecessary. But Level 2 does not cover your specific menu or procedures.
**How to prevent:** Allergen training is mandatory and menu-specific. Run a 15-minute session whenever the menu changes. Record attendance in this tracker.
**If it is already happening:** Schedule a 15-minute allergen refresher at the next team meeting. Use T019-04 to test knowledge afterward.

---

## Customisation Notes

**This tool is a starting point, not a rulebook.**

**Independent / Specialty Cafe:** Add columns for barista skills certification (SCA modules), latte art progression, or brewing method competencies.
**Chain / Franchise:** Map to your brand training matrix. Add brand-specific e-learning module completion dates.
**Kiosk / Mobile:** Simplified version -- focus on Level 2 status and allergen training only.
**Bakery Cafe:** Add bakery hygiene training, proving/baking temperature competency, and decorated goods allergen awareness.

**Making it yours:**
- Print a summary and pin it in the staff area (names and status only -- no personal details)
- Set calendar reminders 3 months before each certificate expiry
- Celebrate team members who complete additional training
- Use the monthly summary to report training compliance to the Owner

The structure matters more than the specific fields. Keep the structure, change the content.

---

## Connected Resources

**Other tools in this block:**
- T019-02 -- New Starter Food Safety Induction Checklist (L1, checklist)
- T019-03 -- Monthly Team Training Session Guide (L2, guide)
- T019-04 -- Food Safety Competency Assessment (L2, assessment)
- T019-05 -- Annual Training Needs Analysis Template (L3, template)

**Related blocks:**
- B017

**Parent block:** B019 -- Food Safety Training Plan
**Module:** M01: Food Safety & Hygiene Compliance
---

*Tool Version 1.0 -- OIS Cafe v2*
*Built for cafe operators who value clarity, consistency, and craft.*
